2606.10794.title: READER: Robust Evidence-based Authorship Decoding via Extracted Representations
2606.10794.paper_summary: As agentic applications increasingly route user tasks through official and third-party LLM APIs, provenance becomes an operational question: which model generated a given black-box response? We study Dynamic Black-Box LLM Provenance: identifying the source LLM from generations elicited by query-varying, non-predefined prompts rather than a fixed input set or benchmark suite. This setting is difficult because prompt semantics dominate the text, while model-specific authorship traces are weak and inconsistent at the surface level. We introduce READER (Robust Evidence-based Authorship Decoding via Extracted Representations), a lightweight provenance framework that treats a frozen proxy LLM as a reader of hidden authorship evidence. READER maps black-box outputs into proxy activation space, temporally filters token states within each response, and performs Bayesian Evidence Accumulation by summing single-response log-posterior evidence across independently sampled prompts. This avoids fragile mean-pooling of prompt-specific representations while preserving the query-wise evidence needed for calibrated confidence. On Agent500, a 50-target dataset built from agent-style prompts, READER reaches $31.0$-$42.4\%$ top-1 accuracy from a single response and $70.0$-$84.0\%$ from 50 responses, substantially outperforming sentence-encoder fingerprints. Scaling across nine proxy readers further shows that stronger LLMs expose more linearly decodable authorship structure, suggesting that authorship perception is already present in frozen LLM representations and can be converted into reliable multi-query attribution.
2606.10794.paper_summary_zh: 隨著代理應用程序越來越多地通過官方和第三方 LLM API 路由用戶任務，來源成為一個操作性問題：哪個模型生成了給定的黑箱響應？我們研究動態黑箱 LLM 來源：從由查詢變化、非預定義提示引發的生成中識別源 LLM，而不是固定的輸入集或基準套件。這種設定很困難，因為提示語義主導文本，而模型特定的著作權痕跡在表面層面上是薄弱且不一致的。我們介紹 READER（通過提取表示的穩健基於證據的著作權解碼），這是一個輕量級的來源框架，將凍結的代理 LLM 視為隱藏著作權證據的讀者。READER 將黑箱輸出映射到代理激活空間，對每個響應中的標記狀態進行時間過濾，並通過在獨立抽樣的提示之間對單一響應的對數後驗證據進行求和來執行貝葉斯證據累積。這避免了脆弱的提示特定表示的均值池化，同時保留了進行校準信心所需的查詢級證據。在 Agent500 上，這是一個由代理風格提示構建的 50 個目標數據集，READER 從單個響應中達到 $31.0$-$42.4\%$ 的 top-1 準確率，從 50 個響應中達到 $70.0$-$84.0\%$，顯著超越了句子編碼器指紋。跨越九個代理讀者的擴展進一步顯示，較強的 LLM 揭示了更多線性可解碼的著作權結構，這表明著作權感知已經存在於凍結的 LLM 表示中，並且可以轉化為可靠的多查詢歸因。
2606.10794.author: Jiaxu Liu et.al.
2606.10794.authors: Jiaxu Liu, Sunnan Mu, Dong Huang, Liuyin Wang, Jing Shao, Jie Zhang
